summary: We are launching two flagship programs—Asia Start and Intelligent China—while racing to finalize the China Going-Global report for a mid-September launch in Singapore, with SEO prioritization and agency selection decisions driving this week's urgency.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the simultaneous launch of Asia Start (with dual AI and traditional campaign routes now live) and the new Intelligent China program, whose webpage must go live this week. We have decided to prioritize SEO for these two programs over US partnerships, and the team is auditing conversion tracking and target audience profiles to support this. The second major initiative is the joint CKGSB-IE report 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global,' targeting a mid-September launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore. This week, Felix must create a one-pager proposal and confirm event partnerships, while the team begins translating the Chinese report and identifying alumni case studies. Urgent this week: Joseph needs to get the Intelligent China webpage ready, Julie must provide website menu feedback and adjust program figures in the brochure, and Kayleigh is following up with Media Minds (the chosen social media agency) and Impactable. Also critical: finalizing the Shun Tian Shu press release with Jimmy incorporating the 10 points, and uploading the Gao Feng article to the Knowledge section by July 1. Upcoming: we will plan SCM campaigns for Intelligent China and start thinking about homepage messaging images, while Ira follows up with Dean Haitao on article approvals.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Strategy
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to improve CKGSB's search ranking for programs in China, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships. They plan to audit existing conversion tracking and analyze target audience profiles.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on website menu, progress on brochures and videos, and challenges with social media agency search. Decided to prioritize Media Minds due to small budget and time constraints.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with op-eds from both schools' leadership and industry case studies, aiming for launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore mid-September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), reviewed results, and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' targeting foreigners, with a focus on module one in Beijing.

meetings.title: Europe-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on geopolitical tensions affecting European companies in China, with emphasis on de-risking and the role of companies versus governments.

decisions.text: Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships for initial SEO efforts.
Intelligent China webpage to launch this week; Asia Star page already live.
Prioritize Media Minds as social media agency due to small budget and time constraints.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, incorporating the 10 points.
IE report translation should not exceed 50% of the original.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Target launch at Singapore events in mid-September (Tech in Asia or Tyson).
Report will include main research feature, op-eds from both schools' leadership, and industry case studies.
Asia Start campaign will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI-driven and traditional.
Intelligent China program will be marketed as a deeper dive compared to Asia Start to avoid cannibalization.
Republish Gao Feng Advisory Pulse article as Professor Analysis on Knowledge section, mentioning original source and Prof Tse's affiliation
decisions.meetingTitle: FW: Gao Feng Advisory Pulse | From Digital Employees to Knowledge Productivity
